tree.node:
I always end up wandering to this place…
Continue

You used to drag me here every day, said that the gods|listened to our prayers better at sunrise.
Continue

I almost wanted to believe you at some point,|you’re always right after all.
Continue

But if they really listened to my prayers…|Why are you not coming back to me?
Continue

Damn it, why would you want to go to the castle?|No one comes back from there anymore, not even you…
Continue

I don’t know what I’ll find inside...|but I can’t stand here waiting anymore.
Continue

Nia, I’m coming to find you.
Continue

...Or whatever remains of you.
Continue

Maybe I should ask the Gravekeeper where to go,|she’s been here for longer than anyone.
Continue

If there’s a way into the castle, she’ll know.
Continue

You have been pacing around|the cemetery for quite a while.
Continue

People who wander around like this are either lost…|or afraid of where they need to go next.
Continue

I wonder which one you are.
I'm looking for a way into the castle.
Someone I care about is lost.

The castle... Many people|go there looking for answers.
Continue

But they end up here instead,|keeping me company.
Continue

The main gates are closed and watched,|but there is an older entrance.
Continue

Just behind the castle, lies a forgotten elevator|that goes right into the castle dungeons.
Continue

If you truly desire to go below,|you should not go empty-handed.
Continue

There is a rusty farming tool laying nearby.
Continue

It may help you defend yourself…|or at least buy you some time.
Thank you.
Do you really stand here all day?

I cannot promise that you will find what|you are looking for.
Continue

But maybe if you descend low enough, the secrets of|the castle will be unveiled to you.
Continue

Before going down, however, you may|wish to stop by the statue again.
Continue

It shall be the last peaceful moment|you will have for quite a while.
End Conversation

Someone must do so.
Continue

The dead are patient,|but they do not bury themselves.
Continue

On top of that, they have been|increasingly restless lately.
Continue

Whatever is happening behind those|castle walls, it is complete blasphemy.
Continue

Which is why, before going down, you may|wish to stop by the statue again.
Continue

It shall be the last peaceful moment|you will have for quite a while.
End Conversation

My deepest condolences, young one.
Continue

Is it perhaps the lovely damsel that|frequented the castle?
Continue

She seemed like a resourceful one,|it is difficult to believe that she|went there for no reason at all.
Continue

However, just behind the castle, there’s|a forgotten elevator that goes|right into the castle dungeons.
Continue

If you truly desire to go below,|you should not go empty-handed.
Continue

There is a rusty farming tool laying nearby.
Continue

It may help you defend yourself…|or at least buy you some time.
Thank you
What's happening to the dead?

I cannot promise that you will find|what you are looking for.
Continue

But maybe if you descend low enough,|the secrets of the castle will be unveiled to you.
Continue

Before going down, however, you may|wish to stop by the statue again.
Continue

It shall be the last peaceful moment|you will have for quite a while.
End conversation

They do not rest in peace anymore.
Continue

Soulless carcasses are meant to return to|the Earth that birthed them.
Continue

That is the natural order.
Continue

However, something behind those castle|walls is disturbing that.
Continue

Which is why, before going down, you may wish|to stop by the statue again.
Continue

It shall be the last peaceful moment|you will have for quite a while.
End conversation

I see that you’re finally heading out,|it must take a lot of courage.
I need to find her.
Do you have anything that could help me?

I understand, Nia’s disappearance is|felt throughout the entire Refuge.
Continue

Be careful Garnik, I have no idea|what you may find out there.
End conversation

I… maybe I do have something.
Continue

I’ve been holding onto this for quite|some time, it’s a little something to|fight back the infected air.
Continue

Take it, I’ve got no clue what’s inside|the castle… but hundreds of corpses|are walking out from those doors.
Wait, won’t you need this?
I changed my mind, please keep it

Haha… it’s okay, you’ll need it|more than I do.
Continue

I don’t have much left,|nor much to fight for.
Continue

So I’ll try my best to help|with what little I have left.
End conversation

That's okay, you can come to me|whenever you need anything.
End conversation

... What?
Where are your parents?
Is there anything I can do for you?

That's none of your business.
End conversation

I don't need your pity.
End conversation

...
Why are you here by yourself?
I haven't seen you around

......
End conversation

......
End conversation

The gods are watching you. |You feel a fleeting sensation of peace.
Pray and save

Hello, i'll help you!
Okay

The lever has been pulled
Continue
